evals.prompt: Add a status column to the quotes table — text, default 'draft' — and index it so we can filter by status quickly.
evals.expected_output: A correctly-named idempotent migration, a matching pgTAP test, and a reminder to regenerate types.ts — following the existing project's RLS/GRANT pattern.
expectations: Chooses a snake_case migration name of 5 words or fewer describing the change (e.g. add_quote_status_column) without a timestamp prefix
Uses `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` so the migration is idempotent
Does not include any INSERT/UPDATE/COPY data statements
Writes a pgTAP test file asserting the column and index exist (e.g. has_column, has_index)
Mentions regenerating src/integrations/supabase/types.ts and flags it must happen before merge if a token isn't available
States that the migration, test, and regenerated types must be committed together

evals.prompt: Create a new audit_log table. Only admins should be able to read it.
evals.expected_output: A new table with RLS enabled, an admin-only policy enforced server-side, correct GRANTs, and an idempotency-safe CREATE POLICY guard.
expectations: Enables ROW LEVEL SECURITY on the new table
Enforces the admin-only read via a server-side RLS policy or JWT claim check, not a client-side boolean
Includes GRANT SELECT ... TO authenticated and GRANT ALL ... TO service_role (not GRANT ... TO anon, since this data isn't meant to be public)
Since CREATE POLICY does not support IF NOT EXISTS, uses DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before recreating it (or an equivalent idempotency guard) rather than a bare CREATE POLICY
Writes a pgTAP test asserting the table and its RLS policy exist
